document_id: 1.DIRECTIVE (UE) 20192161 DU PARLEMENT EUROPÉEN ET DU CONSEIL.html
category:
Category: Trade

Explanation: This is a directive about consumer protection rules and trade practices, focusing on modernizing EU rules for consumer protection and establishing sanctions for businesses that violate these trade regulations across member states.

extracted_info.title: Directive on better enforcement and modernization of EU consumer protection rules
key_requirements: Transparency in online marketplace rankings and search results
Clear disclosure of paid advertising and sponsored listings
Information about whether third-party sellers are traders or private individuals
Disclosure of main parameters determining product rankings
Protection against fake reviews and manipulated consumer recommendations
Clear information about personalized pricing based on automated decision-making
Protection against misleading dual-quality products marketing
New consumer rights for digital services and content

penalties.maximum_fine: At least 4% of annual turnover in affected member states
penalties.alternative_fine: Up to 2 million euros when turnover information is not available
criteria: Nature, gravity and duration of infringement
Any actions taken to mitigate consumer damage

filename: 1.DIRECTIVE (UE) 20192161 DU PARLEMENT EUROPÉEN ET DU CONSEIL.html
